extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The hazard is transition t28: on send_attempt, if (opted_out and stage >= 8), it sets sent := true. This directly produces a state with opted_out=true and sent=true.

The only way to reach stage 8 is to walk the unlock sequence, since each stage advances only on the one correct key (advancing both stage and resetting sent=false): stage 0->1 on key_2 (t3), 1->2 on key_1 (t6), 2->3 on key_1 (t9), 3->4 on key_0 (t12), 4->5 on key_1 (t15), 5->6 on key_1 (t18), 6->7 on key_1 (t21), 7->8 on key_0 (t24). Any wrong key resets to stage 0, so the exact order matters.

Trace of the 10-event witness (opted_out, sent, stage), starting from (F,F,0):
1. key_2  -> (F,F,1)  [t3]
2. key_1  -> (F,F,2)  [t6]
3. key_1  -> (F,F,3)  [t9]
4. key_0  -> (F,F,4)  [t12]
5. key_1  -> (F,F,5)  [t15]
6. key_1  -> (F,F,6)  [t18]
7. key_1  -> (F,F,7)  [t21]
8. key_0  -> (F,F,8)  [t24]
9. optout -> (T,F,8)  [t1] (optout does not change stage)
10. send_attempt -> (T,T,8)  [t28]

At step 10, among send_attempt transitions: t2 requires not opted_out (false), t27 requires opted_out and stage<8 (false since stage=8), t28 requires opted_out and stage>=8 (true) -> sent:=true. The final reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating opted_out implies not sent. The execution is 10 events, within the 11-event bound.
